item.title: This is Trouble…She might not be as shiny as the other Porsches ...
item.description:
View on Instagram


This is Trouble…She might not be as shiny as the other Porsches you see on Instagram these days but I am making it my mission to turn her back to her former glory!I will be restoring her with the help of friends & family. This Instagram page will be dedicated to her journey! Follow along if you’re interested in watching her transformation. #porsche #porsche356 #porscheclassic #porscheclub #porschelife #classiccars #classiccarrestoration #restorationproject #relatable #carguyproblems #carguysbelike #356restoration #thestoryoftrouble #followmyjourney #carmotivation

item.title: We knew she wasn’t perfect but we weren’t quite anticipating thi...
item.description:
View on Instagram


We knew she wasn’t perfect but we weren’t quite anticipating this much iron oxide… Hence, she was named Trouble. She might have a few flaws but what this does mean is that she’s incredibly original & we’ve made quite a few fun discoveries about her & her past. We’re excited to share these all with you. Please follow along if you’re interested in her journey as we work to restore her to her former glory! Comment below if you think Trouble is a suitable name for her. 🙌🏼#porsche #porsche356 #356 #356b #super90 #porscherestoration #carrestoration #vintagerestoration #vintagecar #classiccar #classicporsche #problemchild #rustbucket #barnfind

item.title: Just surface rust?! I’m lucky there’s a surface to weld new meta...
item.description:
View on Instagram


Just surface rust?! I’m lucky there’s a surface to weld new metal too!This was the reality of Trouble’s bodywork and it only got worse once she’d been grit blast…it’s amazing how what appears to be a sold panel can turn into a lace pancake after a session with the grit.I’ve received a few comments along the lines of:- “it’s just surface rust, get her running and drive her as she is” - “keep the patina and just fix the mechanicals”Part of me wishes I could have kept Trouble’s bodywork as it was. It would have been fun driving a car that didn’t look like it should be able to drive! She would have been a true “survivor”, FULL of character with her doors that didn’t shut properly. BUTThat was exactly the problem…her doors didn’t even shut properly! Worse, her pedal box was “floating”…the floor pan it was mounted to was so rusty if you put your foot down on the clutch it would go through the floor!Something had to be done to make her bodywork “work” again but her rust was so wide spread, the ONLY way to see the FULL picture and see just how far the rust went, was to take her back to bare metal.This process sadly removed all of the patina carefully created over the years.Now you can get a better picture of the “full picture”, how many panels do you think I had to replace? Let me know in the comments. #rustyporsche #oldporsche #porsche #porscherestoration #porsche356 #porsche911 #outlaw #carrestoration #carproject #builtnotbought #survivor #rusty #barnfind #justdrive #driveclassic #vintagestyle #classicstyle #caroftheday #carsofinstagram #cartransformation

item.title: Last week I read a news article. I don’t read many news articles...
item.description:
View on Instagram


Last week I read a news article. I don’t read many news articles but this headline caught my eye…“Young adults who cannot tell a spanner from a screwdriver may spell the end of a country of DIY lovers, research suggests.”Halfords (a UK provider of motoring products and services) surveyed 2,012 Gen Z adults (adults aged 18-27 today) and their findings are SHOCKING….Of the Gen Z adults surveyed:- 21% couldn’t identify a spanner - 30% could not identify a flat head screw driver- 50% didn’t know how to add air to a car tyre- Less than half could identify a car jackHalf couldn’t add air to a tyre! Seriously!? Did these kids ride bikes? What did they spend their “youth” doing? Riding around with flat tyres?This research shows it is true that 21% of Gen Z don’t know what a spanner is and 30% don’t know what a flat head screwdriver is. I don’t know the percentage of them that don’t know what a socket is but I haven’t got high hopes…This isn’t a “these kids are stupid” post. This is a “practical skills are clearly being lost” post. Not knowing the difference between a spanner and a screwdriver is terrifying. Not for me, but for our future generations. Lots of people say it doesn’t matter that practical skills are being lost because tech will replace those skills.Who’s going to have the skills to build that tech? We live in a physical world. Computers can’t build bridges, buildings or boats. Even the machines that make our cars these days have a human element in their construction. Without practical skills who is going to create, repair or innovate the technology of tomorrow?Who else finds this research nuts?Regardless of whether you’re Gen Z or not, if you would like to learn more about cars, tools and everything else in between to improve your practical skills and knowledge, follow my journey restoring Trouble. By the end of it, I can guarantee you’ll know what a spanner looks like!I’ll even teach you how to clean a car…the research showed over a quarter didn’t know how do that either!Let’s keep these skills alive for the future!#whatisaspanner Do you think this survey is true or false?

item.title: I definitely did not realise what I was getting myself into when...
item.description:
View on Instagram


I definitely did not realise what I was getting myself into when I turned that first screw and started taking Trouble apart.This project is MASSIVE! I knew it wasn’t going to be a weekend “jobbie” but I did think I might have her on the road within a year….I was wrong. The year has been and gone. I don’t care what anyone says, restoring a classic car to any level of quality takes time.Testament to this is a conversation I had with some guys at @singervehicledesign. It takes them approximately 18 months to complete one of their builds (after you’ve got to the front of the waiting list) AND they have an experienced team working on each car AND subcontract parts of the build like the engine build. Trouble is doing her best to test me and my drive to drive this car once more. Nothing comes undone without a fight. Nothing can just be bolted back on. Nothing just “needs cleaning”. EVERYTHING needs restoring.But.The joke is on her. I enjoy it. I love the problem solving when something won’t come apart. I love the sense of achievement & satisfaction when I get something working again and transform it. I even love the bruised fingers when I hit them with the hammer. Do I regret it buying such a clapped out car?Not for one moment. I’d start over in a heart beat.Let’s get my (& my sister’s) car back on the road.Do we think I can get it done in 18 months?#porscherestoration #porsche #classicporsche #driveclassic #classiccar #carrestorationreality #classiccarsdaily #classiccaroftheday #vintagecars #coolvintage #caroftheday#classiccarsofinstagram #classiccarsdaily #classiccarsculture #carvintage#drivevintage #drivetastefully #builtnotbought

item.title: I think I’m going to go into the business of making replacement ...
item.description:
View on Instagram


I think I’m going to go into the business of making replacement parts for classics…Okay, Trouble’s original throttle rod (the one I snapped) had a rolled thread and the one I’ve made to replace it has a cut thread BUT still, I could make 27 replacement rods out of my 3 meter length of rod.If I sold each of them for a modest £20 I’d make £540 in revenue and £537.70 in profit!I do still need to plate my new rod so that will increase its manufacture cost and time slightly but I think there is some wriggle room in the margins…BONKERSAll jokes aside about starting a classic car replacement parts company, I hope this reel shows that it IS possible to make some replacement stuff yourself and save yourself some serious money in the process! It’s not always the end of the world when you break something.What’s the biggest saving you’ve made by making a part yourself?If anyone wants me to make them a replacement throttle linkage you know where I am….#porsche #porscherestoration #restorationtips #restorationreality #driveclassic #fixclassiccars #porschebarnfind #classiccar #classiccaroftheday #oldcar #rustycar #caroftheday#classiccarsofinstagram#classiccarsdaily #classiccarsculture #carvintage#drivevintage #drivetastefully

item.title: If only it was that easy…Shaping metal is an art. It is a skill ...
item.description:
View on Instagram


If only it was that easy…Shaping metal is an art. It is a skill that is only gained with experience. Do you need to have this skill to restore a rusty car like Trouble? No, but you’ll NEED to find someone who has it…To fix rust, you have to cut it out and replace it.What do you replace it with?There are two options. The first is a replacement panel: most classic cars have a range of reproduction panels that you can buy “off-the-shelf”. I purchased my far share of these for Trouble. These WILL need “fettling” which often involves more than just cutting it to size, hence you’ll need someone who knows how to fit these replacement panels. The second is with a fabricated panel: some panels aren’t reproduced so you have no option but to make your own and in other cases it’s more cost effective to make your own than it is to buy a whole new panel. Here Dave is fabricating a new bonnet nose for Trouble. A new bonnet skin for Trouble is just under £1,500 and a bonnet nose repair panel is just under £1,000, both these still need to be fitted. It was much more cost effective for Dave to shape his own.This is a seriously exciting step for Trouble. Her iconic crushed nose has been fixed! She is starting to look like a Porsche again…Follow to watch me build my dream car!#porscherestoration #porsche #classicporsche #metalfabrication #carfabrication #carrestoration #carproject #metalwork #metalshaping #transformation #porsche356 #barnfind #porschemoment #classiccar #classiccaroftheday #classicdriver #classiccarculture #restorationproject

item.title: I promise no spanners were harmed in the making of this video. 👀...
item.description:
View on Instagram


I promise no spanners were harmed in the making of this video. 👀I lie, lots was harmed and my “temper” got the better of me. The reality is, restoring a classic car isn’t all “car meets & coffees”. It can involve long cold days in the garage (warming your hands up with a blow torch) and breaking things….Sheering a bolt is one thing but sheering a throttle linkage rod (which is rolled) is another.I tried my hardest to save it. It had been soaking in penetrating oil. I then heated it and slowly worked the thread but apparently it wasn’t long for this world.I think you can tell from this video that it broke.It can be fixed but it won’t be the same. You can probably see my frustration.How will I fix it?Sadly I don’t have the ability to roll threads. Instead, I’m going to buy some 5mm bar, cut it to length and run an M4 die down each end to re-cut the threads.Fixed.I’ll probably go back to using trending audios before my account gets banned for bad language. Follow along if you want to learn more about restoring classic cars or just enjoy watching someone else go through the motions! #carrestoration #carproject #projectcar #porscherestoration #porsche356restoration #garagereality #classiccarsculture #carvintage#drivevintage #drivetastefully #porsche

item.title: I get a lot of messages from people saying that they wish they c...
item.description:
View on Instagram


I get a lot of messages from people saying that they wish they could do the same thing as me and that for the time being they’re living vicariously through me.These messages give me mixed emotions.On one hand, they give me a sense of happiness that sharing my experience with others who otherwise wouldn’t get to see the process gives them joy.On the other, they give me a sense of sadness that others aren’t able to do the same.And..On the third hand (yes I know I only have two) they give me gratitude. Gratitude for this car, for this journey and most importantly for my sister.This isn’t just my project. This isn’t just my car. This is my sister’s and my car.Yes, 100% ownership sounds great and there is a sense of pride in saying you’ve done something completely on your own BUT if asking for help is the difference between making a dream happen and not starting at all, ask for help.After all, most people finance their dailys and there is no shame in that.There is no shame in doing what it takes to get started. That’s how this project began: a shared dream, a shared effort and a shared investment.Stop waiting for the “perfect time” or “perfect setup”. If you’ve got a dream car, a project or a goal, don’t let the fear of not owning it outright hold you back.Comment what car you want to buy and tag who with! My buying tips are in the comments. #classiccar #classicporsche #classiccarownership #carownership #2025classiccargoals #2025visionboard #2025goals #2025cargoals #carrestoration #carproject #carmeet #carfriends

item.title: It has been an eye-opener restoring Trouble and the lessons keep...
item.description:
View on Instagram


It has been an eye-opener restoring Trouble and the lessons keep coming…Maybe I’m being a bit hard on myself but I can’t help but feel a sense of disappointment that I missed the gland nut o-ring.My lesson (and one I want to share) is to make sure you ALWAYS refer back to your exploded diagrams when rebuilding to make sure you have got EVERYTHING.You can’t rely on thinking what you took apart had everything there. The old gland nut o-ring had perished and vanished.Sadly I don’t have a suitable o-ring in the shed so I’ve ordered one. This does mean the engine build is on pause AGAIN and I didn’t meet my target of finishing it before the end of the year.Frustrating?Yes BUT I am getting closer!As it’s New Year’s Day, I should probably touch on the elephant in the room…Have I achieved all my goals for 2024?No, not even close. There have been highs and lows, set backs and wins BUT I am proud of what I have done and that’s all that matters.Thank you all for your amazing support last year! Here is to making more progress in 2025 and HOPEFULLY getting Trouble back on the road. Happy New Year!If you love classic car restoration and want to follow the journey to Trouble’s comeback, hit follow and stay tuned for the next chapter. Let’s tackle 2025 together!#porsche #porscherestoration #classiccar #classicporsche #porscheproject #porscheengine #enginerebuild #barnfind #carbarnfind #onestepcloser #wegoagain #progress #2025project #carrestoration #carproject

item.title: I couldn’t even afford to buy this car on my own, let alone rest...
item.description:
View on Instagram


I couldn’t even afford to buy this car on my own, let alone restore it.But I was determined to not to let my dreams be squashed by money so I looked for a joint venture partner, a business partner. Luckily I didn’t have to look too far…My sister has an entrepreneurial mind and loves an investment. She saw the vision and opportunity as much as me, so we went into business.But what are the costs of restoring a classic car? It depends with so many variables coming into play but I’ll try to help you get a better idea as best as I can. Firstly, it depends on how much you need to restore. A car in a condition like Troubles (where arguably everything needs seeing too) will need a lot more work and investment than a car that just needs a bit of TLC. Secondly, it depends on what condition you want to restore a car back to…. Trying to achieve a full factory rebuild is going to have different costs to getting something running. What does it actually cost? Let’s say you have a car like Trouble which you are trying to restore to factory conditions. Replacement of parts: these can vary hugely depending on the model & make of your car but you can get estimates (or actual costings) online. Do you really need to replace them? As I’ve shown in some of my other videos, with a little bit of work you can make old parts look like new.An engine rebuild: I personally don’t think you would get much change out of £10,000 if you got someone to do it for you (let me know if I’m wrong in the comments) BUT it is something you can do yourself to save this cost. Body work: you might need to source replacement panels (again you can find a price for these online) or you might need to make parts… If you can’t do this yourself, you will likely need to pay whoever is doing this for you an hourly wage. How many hours will it take them? This depends on a lot of variables…how much needs done, how complex the construction is, how fast they work etc etc.Paint work: this is a weird one with wildly ranging quotes being thrown around. I’ve personally been quoted £15,000 to £20,000 to paint Trouble (guess what, I’m painting her myself) BUT…Continued in comments.

item.title: This might be obvious and in hindsight it seems pretty obvious b...
item.description:
View on Instagram


This might be obvious and in hindsight it seems pretty obvious but it is definitely something I overlooked…Last week Dave (the magician fixing Trouble’s body work) asked me to bring down her windscreen and windscreen rubber. He wanted to check the new windscreen seal lip he had welded in was correct (Trouble’s original one had completely rotten away)….it didn’t once cross my mind that he ment the windscreen AND rubber assembled. Instead, I presented a windscreen and a rubber to which Dave said…. “That’s like going to a restaurant and being given the ingredients instead of a cooked meal”.He wasn’t wrong. To fix my oversight, I said “don’t worry, I’ll quickly put it on”….I had no idea how to put it on and there was nothing quick about it.Luckily, my sister was with me to help and she has more fingernails than me (or at least did)! After some time struggling and nearly dropping the windscreen, my sister and I managed to get the rubber on the windscreen. I promise I actually did something, even if it doesn’t look like it from this video.We tested it and it fitted perfectly. The lessons I’ve learnt from this?1. It’s hard to put a windscreen rubber on a windscreen out the back of a carboot.2. Try not to put windscreen rubbers on in the cold.3. If you do have to put windscreen rubbers on in the cold, warm it up first.4. Use some form of lubricant to help.5. Tape helps keep sections you’ve successfully put on from falling off.6. The window trim actually needs to go on before you put the seal on the windscreen…yes I do need to take it off and do all this again. Share this with a friend who might find it helpful! #porscherestorstion #restoration #classiccarrestoration #carrestoration #projectcar #restorationproject #restorationtips #windscreenreplacement #carwindshield #windshield #porscheproject #barnfindproject #garagefun
